0.tagline: Persistent computers for agents, made tangible.
0.summary: An interactive launch experience for Reticle, Dedalus's persistent Linux computers for AI agents. It turns sub-second startup, durable runtime state, and metered compute into a product story people can understand by using it.

1.tagline: A phone-first agent for one very live room.
1.summary: Ariadne was the conversational layer for Dedalus's Run(way)time event at Lume Studios. Guests texted it to check in, receive identities and secret words, complete quests, request songs, and order drinks without installing an app.

2.tagline: Cloud sandboxes put under visible pressure.
2.summary: A public head-to-head benchmark for code-execution sandboxes. Providers run the same open workloads side by side while the arena streams timing, reliability, and resource results, then combines objective scores with crowd Elo.

3.tagline: A birthday archive built like a digital relic.
3.summary: An interactive twenty-first-birthday shrine combining liquid-chrome typography, cinematic monochrome imagery, an explorable labyrinth, photo archives, and a hidden reliquary into one deeply personal web experience.

4.tagline: A personal knowledge base that stays source-first.
4.summary: A compiled personal wiki spanning architecture, agent operations, tools, skills, projects, and philosophy. Flat Markdown remains canonical while the interface adds instant search, native diagrams, a knowledge graph, and a corpus-aware Wikibot.

5.summary: Agent Machines provisions persistent Linux environments for coding agents, with durable filesystems, resumable state, and tool access. Work survives beyond a single session instead of disappearing when the conversation ends.

6.tagline: The operating layer for agents that need a real computer.
6.summary: Dedalus builds fast, persistent Linux machines for agentic workloads. The platform gives agents durable compute, filesystems, networking, and tools so they can inhabit an environment rather than make isolated function calls.

7.tagline: A design system humans and agents can share.
7.summary: An agent-first interface system with more than 350 React components, 46 visual presets, and one token-driven control layer for color, typography, radius, and motion. It is designed for both direct human use and programmatic composition.

8.tagline: Year one of Dedalus, hidden inside a CRT.
8.summary: A nostalgic terminal experience celebrating Dedalus's first birthday. Visitors type commands at DCD Terminal v1.9, inspect a cluttered digital desk, browse polaroid memories, and unlock hidden moments from the company's first year.

9.tagline: Skills that update when their sources change.
9.summary: Loop monitors documentation, changelogs, and repositories used by AI-agent skills. It researches upstream changes, produces reviewable diffs, and rewrites the relevant playbooks so operational knowledge does not quietly go stale.

10.tagline: Defend Princeton, one campus at a time.
10.summary: A canvas-based tower-defense game set across Princeton's campus, with 26 hand-built maps, seven tower classes, nine playable heroes, and more than 100 enemy types rendered through a custom browser game loop.

11.summary: An interactive demo of the Dedalus SDK for building model-agnostic agents with MCP. It makes tool orchestration, server connections, authentication, and multi-tenant execution visible through working product flows.

12.tagline: A control room for hackathon judging.
12.summary: A judging and event-management platform built for HackPrinceton. Podium coordinates judge assignments, aggregates scores, protects against spam and abuse, and publishes live results without forcing organizers into spreadsheet triage.

13.tagline: Research without losing the citation trail.
13.summary: A research workspace for discovering, annotating, and synthesizing academic papers. Sevenfold combines semantic search, inline notes, corpus-wide conversation, and citation tracing in a single evidence-preserving workflow.

14.tagline: Forty real projects, compiled into a battle game.
14.summary: A fully playable portfolio inspired by competitive monster battlers. Every fighter is a shipped project with its own types, stats, signature moves, status effects, team strategy, and animated battle presentation.

15.tagline: Useful context before the model sees the prompt.
15.summary: Lumachor ingests documents, chunks and embeds them, retrieves relevant passages through vector search, and assembles stronger context for language-model prompts without asking users to become prompt engineers.

16.tagline: A flagship hackathon site built for launch energy.
16.summary: The Fall 2025 HackPrinceton site paired a high-motion public landing page with practical event information and applicant flows. Animated storytelling, schedules, sponsors, and responsive components shared one seasonal system.

17.tagline: Shared expenses resolved in real time.
17.summary: A collaborative expense tracker for groups. Splitway records purchases, calculates who owes whom, updates every participant instantly through Firebase, and keeps settlement state synchronized across connected devices.

18.tagline: Probability tables turned into a playable toy.
18.summary: A browser lootbox simulator with weighted drop tables, rarity-specific collections, animated reveals, multiple box pools, and persistent inventory. The probabilities are the mechanic rather than something hidden behind it.

19.tagline: Princeton campus life as an idle game.
19.summary: A Princeton-themed clicker inspired by incremental classics. Players earn Paw Points, buy campus-specific generators, unlock upgrades, climb prestige tiers, and keep progress locally between sessions.

20.tagline: A reusable spring identity for HackPrinceton.
20.summary: The Spring 2025 HackPrinceton site translated the season's visual identity into a responsive component system, animated public pages, clear event information, and an integrated application path.

21.tagline: The first system in a multi-season event series.
21.summary: The Fall 2024 HackPrinceton site established the structure later seasons inherited: sponsor showcases, live schedules, FAQs, application routing, and a forty-eight-hour countdown wrapped in a coherent event identity.

22.tagline: A calibrated eye chart that runs on ordinary screens.
22.summary: A low-cost visual-acuity testing tool that recreates the Snellen exam on a calibrated display. It guides users through the protocol and produces a portable result without requiring specialized clinic hardware.

23.tagline: Turn what is already in the fridge into dinner.
23.summary: LetMeCook uses computer vision to identify available ingredients, then generates step-by-step recipes shaped by dietary needs, cuisine preferences, and meal type instead of starting from a blank search box.

24.tagline: A study guide assembled around the actual text.
24.summary: Balladeer converts a literary work into structured chapter summaries, character analysis, themes, contextual quotations, and essay prompts, giving students a navigable study artifact instead of a single chat response.

25.tagline: A clearer starting point for navigating U.S. services.
25.summary: CompassUSA organizes legal aid, ESL programs, healthcare access, government services, and procedural guides for immigrants by location and need, with multilingual paths through unfamiliar systems.

26.tagline: Accessible overnight apnea monitoring from inexpensive hardware.
26.summary: A wearable prototype that combines Arduino sensors with live data streaming to monitor blood oxygen and breathing patterns, flag likely apnea events, and surface overnight trends on a companion dashboard.

27.tagline: Following money and influence through the iron triangle.
27.summary: An interactive history project tracing U.S. defense spending, lobbying, and contractor relationships. Animated diagrams and scroll-led narrative make a dense military-industrial system legible without flattening it.

28.tagline: Branching fiction generated one consequential choice at a time.
28.summary: An interactive-fiction engine where a premise becomes a branching story in real time. AdventureGPT preserves narrative state, adapts to player decisions, supports genre constraints, and exports the resulting journey.

29.tagline: Code review built directly into the editor loop.
29.summary: An in-browser editor that pairs syntax-aware writing with AI review for bugs, style, performance, and maintainability. Suggestions arrive as inspectable diffs with direct refactoring paths.

30.summary: The official OMMC test portal handles timed assessment delivery, answer submission, proctoring controls, live scoring, and leaderboard generation for large concurrent cohorts.

31.tagline: Practice under the same pressure as the real competition.
31.summary: A preparation environment for OMMC with past problems, realistic timing, instant scoring, solution walkthroughs, and performance feedback that lets students rehearse the complete competition loop.

32.tagline: An early, deliberately minimal interface for frontier models.
32.summary: A fast GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 client with conversation history, model switching, configurable system prompts, and token tracking. Enkrateia explored how little interface an effective model workspace actually needs.

33.tagline: Speech recognition tuned for voices generic models miss.
33.summary: A transcription system trained around speech patterns associated with Huntington's disease. Its custom acoustic modeling targets dysarthric speech that general-purpose transcription systems frequently fail to recognize.

34.tagline: A worldwide math competition with its own digital home.
34.summary: The official website for the Online Monmouth Math Competition, supporting registration, problem archives, sponsors, updates, and community information across multiple competition seasons.

35.tagline: Every OMMC problem, searchable as one corpus.
35.summary: A searchable database for the full OMMC problem archive. Atlas supports filtering by year, round, difficulty, and topic, renders mathematical notation, hosts solution discussion, and includes an administrative publishing surface.

36.tagline: Computer vision that physically sorts waste.
36.summary: A PennApps hardware winner that uses OpenCV on a Raspberry Pi to identify waste in real time, then actuates servo-controlled bins to route items into recycling, compost, or trash.

37.tagline: STEM lessons designed to be seen, not only read.
37.summary: An interactive learning platform for mathematics and physics with animated diagrams, progressive explanations, practice problems, worked solutions, and topic-based progress tracking.

38.tagline: Tutors, availability, and booking in one marketplace.
38.summary: A tutoring marketplace that connects students with tutors by subject, availability, and location. Profiles, ratings, search, booking, messaging, authentication, and Firestore data form the complete service loop.

39.tagline: The first web app—and the project that started everything.
39.summary: A satellite assembly game where players choose solar panels, thrusters, antennas, and other components before attempting a launch. Satellite Crafter is the first project in the archive and the one that made building software feel addictive.
